title: Crunch Time Fitness
description: Throughout the class students will participate in workouts that require no equipment and very little time. Workouts will change every class to keep things fresh. By the end of the course students will be able to design their own at home workout to stay in shape when they just don't have time to get to the gym!

title: Yoga Flow
description: A vinyasa (meaning "to place in a special and progressive way") style class, where sequences exemplify a movement with breath connection.  Students will also learn more about yoga's subtle energies to further unite body, mind and heart, enabling deeper self-discovery and a more present, joyful and healthy life.

title: Alverno College Choir
description: Some choir and music reading background is helpful, but not required.  The Women's Choir offers students from all areas of study an opportunity to learn and perform choral music for women's voices. The chorus performs one concert each semester as well as performing at various local and college functions. (Non-music majors welcome)

title: Self Defense for Women
description: Empower yourself by raising your safety awareness and learning self-defense techniques. This class will use hands on instruction. Some physical activity is required.

title: Career Exploration in Science: Math
description: In this course students will engage and network with guest speakers (mostly women and Alverno alums) from various STEM professions in the exploration of STEM career paths.  Students will research different STEM careers and use self-assessment to identify strengths and interests.  Information from guest speakers will be used to identify appropriate professional experiences (internships, summer research, graduate school) to enhance career preparation.

title: Major Decisions
description: In this course, students who are undecided or open to exploring major options will be guided through activities that will explicitly examine interests, values, strengths, and how those are all connected potentially to the world of work. Activities will include value, interest and strengths assessments, exploration of various College majors/minors, and occupational research with an emphasis on how to make well-informed "major" decisions which lead to career and life satisfaction.

title: Mindful Movement
description: Students will explore a variety of gentle, mindful movement techniques which promote relaxation, mindful awareness and creative expression. Included will be practice in the Chinese art of qigong which improves balance, concentration and overall well-being. We will also explore music with free-form movement as a tool for developing peaceful and creative strategies for self-care.

title: Mental Health Aid
description: This course allows students to explore in depth a topic featured in professional research and training in the field of social work and social services, such as those emphasized in the surrounding area of Milwaukee and the state of Wisconsin. Topics for the course will rotate according to assessments of need and based on feedback from the community, including the Social Work Advisory Council. Sample topics may include Trauma Informed Care, Child and Family Welfare, Violence against Women, Racial Social Justice, Death and Dying, and Health Care Services Access.

title: Topics Course
description: This course allows students to explore the topic of mental health.   This one credit course teaches students a beginning understanding of how to identify, understand, and respond regarding mental health issues.  This course also allows students to attend the Mental Health Summit at Alverno College.  Students will experience various learning styles:  PowerPoints, Videos, Class Discussion, Role Plays, Readings, Small Group Discussion, and Self-Reflections. Assignments:  A review of a website prior to the first class, attendance at both in person classes, and active participation. Class sessions will have a professional workshop/conference feel. Two dates of the class:  2/17/24 and 2/24/24.   All majors are welcome.

title: Alverno Success: Leveraging Your Strengt
description: Adjusting to and succeeding in college encompasses personal, academic, and financial elements. Learning how to meet college expectations includes learning how to: 1) participate in academic courses and approach academic assignments; 2) develop a budget to meet financial obligations; and 3) develop a time management plan that makes all these college requirements achievable - and even enjoyable.  This course is thus designed to give students opportunities to share questions and develop understanding about their purpose, goals, and direction as a new college student. It will also provide them with an introduction to some key skills that will help them navigate and manage their college academics and finances.

title: Independent Study
description: Wellness Independent Study course

title: Cardio Pulmanary Resuscitation for Health Care-American Heart Assn Basic Life Support
description: This class is taught by certified American Heart Association (AHA) trainers and utilizes the AHA Basic Life Support (BLS) for the Healthcare program. The course teaches students the 7 steps to effective high-quality Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR), How to use an Automatic External Defibrillator (AED) with CPR, Hands-on chest compressions, and Rescue breathing for Adults, Children, and Infants.Upon successful completion of the full training course, learners receive a 2-year BLS certificate with anytime, anywhere digital access to the certificate and training history through the AHA. This class is for first-time certification and renewal of certification in compliance with AHA standards. Students demonstrate through self assessment the connection between the course content and the outcomes of their graduate program. The course fee covers all associated costs, including the AHA Student Workbook, the registration and course processing, the certification completion card (if successful), and the online course fee for the hybrid option.
